summary: Round-3 debate: the no-UB safety envelope under fixed directive D1 (races/UAF impossible) — unsafe hatch, check policy, FFI attenuation, AI proof burden

positionAssessments.position: P1 — No unsafe escape hatch; residue relocated to human-maintained toolchain primitives plus the audited FFI wall
positionAssessments.strengths: Post-critique, P1 retains three real assets. (1) It is the only position under which D1 reads as an unconditional theorem over all writer-emittable source, and its writer-facing invariant — the steady-state AI writer never authors trust — survives every attack and should be adopted by ANY final form. (2) It correctly prices D0: the human-expert justification for Rust-style unsafe is gone, verbosity is free, and F006/A005/trap=abort give a partial (loop-shaped) payoff-recovery story that refutes 'no hatch forfeits everything' for the carded cases. (3) Its concessions were the most honest in the debate (relocation-not-elimination, frontier size, policy-fence fragility), and its diagnosis that round-2 floors delegitimize the asserted-but-unproven tier is correct as far as it goes.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Five critic hits land and are verified against the corpus. (1) The headline is definitional: P1's own retained constructs (FFI extern declarations with declared fact-attenuation, safe-signatured primitive imports) ARE believe-me constructs in accepted-program source — same semantic kind, different authorship policy — so 'no source construct whose semantics is believe-me' is false as stated. (2) It concentrates ALL residual pressure on the single least-carded, least-checkable channel: the FFI wall is UNDESIGNED, zero primary-source cards (ffi_abi_runtime priority-high), threat-model verify='no' — strictly worse observability than in-language unsafe ('sometimes no'), and P1 concedes writer FFI-wrapping is the reroute channel. (3) Internal contradiction: 'residue small in kind, stable in form' is uncarded and contradicts both the corpus's repeated ruling that frontier size is THE decisive unmeasured variable and P1's own concession 2. (4) The F006/SPARK precedent is overclaimed past card scope — F006 attests the restrictions, not the boundary form; SPARK's real escape (SPARK_Mode-off regions, uncarded but plausible) resembles an in-language declared hatch. (5) Permanent human authorship of the hottest code needs a D0 boundary ruling P1 never requests, and the type-level hatch (interior-mutability capability needed for its own mutex/atomic primitives' safe signatures) is unaddressed — round2-memory-automation bars admitting such a type before the misleading-comparisons-#2 tension is carded. Also stale: it argues from C004-as-provisional; C004 is now CONFIRMED_WITH_AMENDED_SOURCES (2026-07-02).

positionAssessments.position: P2 — Block-scoped unsafe regions with machine-readable per-fact obligation manifests, AI-writable, debug-checked where checkable
positionAssessments.strengths: P2 contributes the correct ARTIFACT SHAPE, which the leading candidate absorbs: per-fact named obligations drawn from a soundness-obligation ledger (the round1-ir-strategy artifact ordered 'under any outcome'), content-addressed identity, generation provenance, rationale, and a bare/manifest-free block made unrepresentable. This is the direct instantiation of the round-1 violation-semantics leading candidate (per-fact marked contexts, debug-checkable, conservative fallback) at the boundary, and it rides infrastructure round 2 already mandates (canonical bytes, provenance mechanism 2, machine-readable trap reports, contract-edit gating). It is also right that round-2 surface text (modules line 15/42, synthesis line 59, memory-automation's 'localized greppable escape-hatch policy whose density is measured') anticipated an in-language boundary construct, and right that N001 argues for localization and naming even where blast radius cannot be semantically bounded.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its two load-bearing arguments fail under verification. (1) Spine violation: for the highest-value obligation classes (whole-execution noalias disjointness, purity, Send/Sync claims) no dynamic check is carded — P2 concedes this — so a manifest entry there is stated-and-AUDITED: the banned third state renamed, requiring a formal spine amendment P2 never requests. (2) The redundancy claim contradicts round 2's own rationale: body and manifest are same-pass, same-writer output — the exact error-correlated condition redundancy channels exist to defeat — and mechanism 2 re-runs generators; it cannot falsify an assertion's truth. Contract-edit gating catches re-edits, not initially coherent body+manifest theater. (3) A005 channel confusion confirmed: it cards RELEASE-mode versioning legality, not a debug obligation-checking tier; the debug tier for aliasing-class obligations is currently vacuous per the corpus's own gap list. (4) 'Existence already entailed' fails on precedence and chronology: user-directives.md, the controlling document, updated after the synthesis, explicitly delegates existence/form to this round. (5) Free writer authorship of UB-capable regions is the weakest possible fit with the decided contract-edit-gating floor — the a-fortiori argument P3 makes against it is sound even though P3's own positive construction is not.

positionAssessments.position: P3 — Toolchain-gated unsafe: core-surface hatch with defined per-fact obligations; authoring/editing is a privileged, quota'd, reviewed toolchain operation; steady-state writer structurally cannot emit it
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Three critic hits are confirmed, partly out of P3's own mouth. (1) 'Gate authorization restores two-state discipline' is a redefinition of 'checked': review — human or AI — is a trusted assertion with the trust relocated, and P3's concessions 3-4 (gate is toolchain policy not type invariant; reviewer independence unresolved, same-model review shares correlated errors) concede exactly what the core argument denies. (2) The a-fortiori from contract-edit gating is disanalogous: the decided gate protects an EXISTING mechanical checker (declarations re-verified against bodies every build); a gated unsafe channel substitutes process for verification for precisely the obligations with no checker behind them. The honest inference is 'at least as restricted', which does not privilege gating over prohibition or proof-discharge. (3) Quota-as-measurement is self-defeating: a capped, latency-priced channel measures demand under the cap while P3's own risk list says pressure reroutes to safe-code contortion and the FFI wall. Additionally, acceptance becomes time- and policy-relative (gate policy, keyring, ledger version), leaving D1's 'accepted programs' quantifier without a stable artifact-checkable predicate; and every gate-efficacy claim is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, with gating-prevents-laundering itself an explicitly listed untested experiment (round2-modules follow-up d).
positionAssessments.strengths: P3 contributes the correct AUTHORSHIP ARCHITECTURE, which the leading candidate adopts as its skeleton. (1) Its Step-1 existence argument is the debate's soundest: C004 (now confirmed) base impls, the threat model's unverifiable rows, and round1-concurrency's 'trusted-base placement, not elimination' jointly show something hatch-shaped must be authored somewhere; keeping it in-language preserves partial verifiability (threat-model 'sometimes no') that P1's FFI relocation forfeits ('no'), and keeps the residue inside the verifier, provenance, and debug machinery. (2) It is the only position that EXTENDS a decided floor (contract-edit gating) rather than contradicting one, and the only one that makes the writer structurally incapable of emitting UB-capable code while retaining an in-language home with defined semantics. (3) It correctly identifies that the backlog's numeric-semantics item independently demands the unchecked tier be 'unsafe/proof-gated', and its request for an explicit owner ruling on D1's meaning is the debate's most procedurally correct move — the critic confirms that ruling arbitrates everything.

keyTradeoffs: Where the third state lives, not whether: all three positions relocate the checked-vs-trusted residue rather than eliminate it — outside the language at toolchain/FFI (P1: invisible to the verifier, zero-card wall), in writer-authored audited manifests (P2: visible but same-pass-correlated), or in a gated process channel (P3: writer-inaccessible but process-trusted). The relocations cannot be compared on evidence until the FFI wall is designed and debug-checkability is carded.

D1's quantifier is the fork: read as an unconditional theorem over all accepted source, only P1 approximates it (modulo an unboundedly-growing TCB and an undesigned wall); read as the conditional guarantee its own reference bar (F001 safe-Rust) actually provides, P2/P3-class confined-obligation forms are admissible. This is a pending normative owner ruling (the backlog already demands it), not an evidence question.

In-language residue preserves partial verifiability and observability (threat-model 'sometimes no', debug checks, provenance, trap reports) at the cost of a conditional headline; out-of-language residue preserves the clean writer-emittable-source theorem at the cost of pushing identical code where verification is 'no' and pressure reroutes silently.

Mechanical vs process discharge: static proof and runtime checks are the only spine-literal states; review is relocated trust. D0 makes proof burden admissible, so prover-gated discharge dominates review wherever obligations are formalizable — the residual channel's size (unformalizable obligations) is unknown and is what frontier sizing must measure.

One boundary family vs per-channel ledgers: if unsafe regions, FFI externs, and primitive imports carry separate obligation regimes, performance pressure arbitrages to the cheapest channel (P1's own FFI-reroute risk); a single family with one ledger closes arbitrage but couples the hatch design to the undesigned FFI wall, making it a blocking dependency.

Frontier size prices every position symmetrically: small → P1's primitive set is a standard library and the gated channel is near-empty; large → the primitive set becomes the feared 'second evidence-free language', manifests metastasize into the program, and the gate becomes the throughput bottleneck for exactly the code the language exists for.

Decided floors cut the hatch's cost asymmetrically in favor of a small hatch (uncounted by all advocates): no unwinding removes exception-safety obligations, closed-world removes interposition falsification, mono-only removes shared-generic F003 discharge — a round-3 hatch is materially cheaper to specify and audit than Rust's.

Acceptance stability: process-dependent acceptance (gate policy, ledger version, keyring state) makes 'accepted program' a time-relative predicate; artifact-checkable acceptance (proof objects, check instrumentation in the binary) keeps D1's quantifier stable but demands the prover-gated machinery nobody argued.
evidenceGaps: Frontier sizing — the single decisive unmeasured variable, named by round1-aliasing, round2-memory-automation's reopening clause, and the backlog: unsafe/UnsafeCell/Arc/arena-index density in performance-critical Rust and SPARK ownership/proof discharge rates. It decides P1-vs-gated-channel emphasis, gate throughput viability, and manifest proliferation risk simultaneously.

FFI wall: zero primary-source cards (ffi_abi_runtime missing_primary_source, priority-high), fact-attenuation UNDESIGNED yet a named soundness precondition — the channel where P1 concentrates ALL residue and where every position's pressure-reroute risk lands. The hatch verdict is not end-to-end evaluable until it exists.

Debug-mode dynamic checkability uncarded (round1-violation-semantics): no Miri/UBSan/TSan-class cards on detecting false noalias/inbounds/nsw/purity/Send assertions or their cost — decides whether manifest/gated obligations are mechanically dischargeable or collapse to review-only, i.e., whether the third state can actually be closed for the obligations that matter.

Prover-gated form uncarded: SPARK proof-discharge rates and its actual boundary form (SPARK_Mode mixing), RustBelt-class semantic obligation formalization, proof-carrying-code governance precedents — the only spine-literal discharge mechanism has no cards, and the critic is right that the option space was truncated exactly there.

All AI-writer behavioral claims EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED (round-2 standing flag, zero AI-codegen evidence): hallucinated-assertion rates, manifest theater, gate routing/laundering behavior, proof-obligation discharge ability with prover assistance — these arbitrate no-hatch vs manifest vs gate vs prover-gated directly.

Interior-mutability vs shared-reference-noalias tension uncarded (misleading-comparisons #2; round2-memory-automation blocks admitting any shared-mutable type before it is carded) — the TYPE-level half of the escape envelope (UnsafeCell-analog capability needed for every position's mutex/atomic/channel signatures) is a blocking sub-decision no advocate placed.

Re-validation triggers undesigned for all forms: whole-program regeneration rewrites the safe code around hash-stable boundary regions whose context-dependent assertions (noalias, non-escape, Send) can be falsified by new callers while every provenance check passes; no card covers re-validation on caller drift.
Trap semantics under racing threads unspecified (round-2 constraint on violation-semantics) — caps the failure story of every position's check tier at the boundary.

No card on defect density of centralized vetted primitive layers vs distributed audited unsafe regions, nor on checker/TCB implementation-defect history — P1's audit-economics core and every TCB-growth argument remain structural reasoning.

Normative gap, not research: the owner ruling on D1's quantifier / the meaning of 'soundness may not be sacrificed' (backlog numeric-semantics follow-up, which already notes any unchecked mode 'must be unsafe/proof-gated') is pending and alone arbitrates P1 vs the confined-obligation family.
verdicts.leadingCandidate: P3 amended — a single gated fact-boundary construct family, absorbing P2's manifest form and P1's writer invariant, with prover-gated discharge as the preferred mechanism. Concretely: the language has exactly ONE boundary family in core surface — unsafe regions, FFI extern regions, and trusted primitive/capability-base imports — sharing one per-fact soundness-obligation ledger (the round1-ir-strategy artifact). A bare or manifest-free member is unrepresentable (P2's form). The steady-state AI writer can neither author nor edit any member: authorship is a privileged, gated toolchain operation extending the decided contract-edit-gating floor (P3's channel), so no writer-emittable believe-me construct exists (P1's invariant, preserved). Every ledger entry must be discharged mechanically wherever possible — static proof, A005-style runtime check under trap=abort, or a machine-checked proof artifact (the critic's undebated prover-gated fourth form, preferred because D0 makes proof burden admissible and it is the only discharge that satisfies the verification spine literally) — with review-only gate authorization confined to obligations lacking any mechanical discharge, quota-logged and density-measured per round2-memory-automation's requirement. Whether that review-only residual is near-empty (collapsing toward P1's primitive set) or substantial (P3's operating regime) is decided by frontier sizing and the pending D1-quantifier ruling, not by this verdict. So the answer to the topic question: YES, a hatch exists — as a gated, obligation-carrying boundary-construct family; NO free-form writer-emittable unsafe block exists in any admissible design.
verdicts.rationale: Decisive where forced. (1) EXISTENCE is forced: pure elimination is incoherent — round1-concurrency finds every design 'bootstraps on permanently-trusted unsafe shared-memory code' (placement, not elimination); C004 (verified CONFIRMED_WITH_AMENDED_SOURCES 2026-07-02, retiring a hedge all three positions carried) requires someone to author Send/Sync-asserting base impls; the threat model has compiler-unverifiable rows; and P1's own retained constructs (FFI externs, primitive imports) are members of the same believe-me family, so its 'no' is authorship policy, not ontology. Round2-modules (lines 15/42), the ir-strategy needed-under-any-outcome artifact, and memory-automation's escape-hatch-policy requirement all independently order this construct family into core surface. User-directives.md (controlling, updated after the synthesis) genuinely delegates existence/form — so P2's 'already entailed' argument fails procedurally — but on the merits the delegation resolves to existence-of-family. (2) NO FREE HATCH is forced: the verification spine ('no third state'), the contract-edit-gating floor a fortiori, N001's unbounded blast radius (round 1's most solid surviving point), and D0's least-trusted-writer premise jointly ban any writer-emittable unchecked assertion; notably, no advocate actually defended one. (3) FORM: the critic's sharpest tension is confirmed — all three positions relocate the third state and rename it (TCB / stated-and-audited / gate-checked). The floors then rank the relocations rather than leaving a tie: mechanical discharge (proof or runtime check) is the only spine-literal state and D0 makes proof burden admissible, so prover-gated discharge dominates review wherever obligations are formalizable; the residue must stay in-language (threat-model 'sometimes no' beats FFI 'no'; P1's relocation concentrates everything on the zero-card undesigned wall, its worst-audited channel) and must be writer-inaccessible (P2's same-pass manifests fail round 2's own error-correlation rationale). That selects the amended-P3 synthesis. Honest where thin: this is design_candidate_later, not a decision, because hardening is blocked by (a) the pending normative owner ruling on D1's quantifier — unconditional theorem vs Rust-class conditional — which the backlog already demands and which alone arbitrates P1 vs the candidate; (b) the undesigned FFI wall; (c) unmeasured frontier size; (d) uncarded debug checkability and prover-gated precedents; (e) the uncarded type-level interior-mutability hatch every advocate ignored. It is not research_needed on the round-1 pattern: unlike violation-semantics, this debate ran on the current verified corpus, and the mechanism-family question has a clear floor-forced leader — what remains is sizing, sub-design, and one owner ruling. All AI-writer behavioral claims herein remain EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED per the round-2 standing flag.
followUps: Obtain the owner ruling on D1's quantifier (unconditional over all accepted source vs conditional-on-confined-mechanically-discharged-obligations) via the existing backlog numeric-semantics follow-up ('meaning of soundness may not be sacrificed'); this blocks final form selection between P1-collapse and the gated family, and should also rule the D0 boundary question (may humans author toolchain primitives / review gate requests, given review is not writing).

Design the boundary-construct family as ONE artifact: unsafe regions + FFI extern regions + primitive/capability imports sharing a single per-fact obligation ledger; discharge the ffi_abi_runtime backlog (priority-high) FIRST, since the wall is every position's pressure sink and the ledger cannot be written without its fact-attenuation semantics; add the reflection capability grants from round2-modules to the same family.

Run frontier sizing (adopts round1-aliasing/memory-automation follow-up): unsafe/UnsafeCell/Arc/arena-index density in performance-critical Rust and SPARK ownership/proof discharge rates, with pre-committed thresholds that select between primitive-set emphasis (small frontier) and gated-channel emphasis (large frontier), and that trigger memory-automation's reopening clause.

Card debug-mode dynamic checkability (Miri/UBSan/TSan-class) for false noalias/inbounds/nsw/purity/Send assertions and its cost — this single gap decides whether ledger obligations are mechanically dischargeable or review-only, i.e., whether the spine can be satisfied literally at the boundary.

Card the prover-gated form's precedents and develop it as a full position in the next round: SPARK proof-discharge rates and its actual SPARK_Mode boundary form, RustBelt-class semantic obligations for unsafe code, proof-carrying-code governance; the critic is right that the option space was truncated at exactly the corpus-supported point.

Card the interior-mutability vs shared-reference-noalias tension (misleading-comparisons #2) and design the type-level capability construct (UnsafeCell-analog) as a named blocking sub-decision of the hatch — the escape envelope is type-shaped as well as block-shaped, and every position's mutex/atomic/channel signatures need it.

Run the AI-writer boundary experiments alongside the flagship drift experiment: inject known-unsound regions and measure manifest-mismatch detection rate, vacuous-rationale rate, gate-request behavior under quota pressure, laundering attempts against gated authorship (extends round2-modules follow-up d), and prover-assisted obligation-discharge success — the data that arbitrates review-only vs proof-gated residue.

Design re-validation triggers for context-dependent boundary assertions under whole-program regeneration (noalias/non-escape/Send falsified by new callers while hashes and provenance pass) — an undesigned requirement for ALL forms, generalizing P3's self-raised risk.

Sequence the round-3 safety-envelope rulings: decide bounds/overflow runtime-check policy BEFORE finalizing the hatch (checked-by-default under trap=abort shrinks the hatch's residual domain to precisely the aliasing/lifetime/capability class with no carded dynamic check), and specify trap meaning under racing threads (round-2 constraint) before the check tier of ledger obligations is fixed.

Record in the decision log the formal amendments this verdict makes: round2-modules follow-up's 'unsafe regions' placeholder and memory-automation's escape-hatch-policy line are instantiated by the gated family; P1's no-hatch reading remains admissible only as the small-frontier collapse of the candidate, not as a separate architecture.

positionAssessments.position: P1 — Always-check with aggressive elimination (mandatory runtime check for every unproven checkable fact in all build modes; elimination only by proof; identical debug/release semantics)
positionAssessments.strengths: For D1-critical fact classes its semantics is the only one derivable from decided law without amendment: D1 (non-debatable) plus the verification spine's two-source rule plus trap=abort jointly force 'unproven bounds/alias fact => check or prove or reject, never ambient assume', and P1's compare+branch-to-abort lowering is exactly the branch-based shape round2-error-handling blessed. Tested-artifact=shipped-artifact is the strongest predictability property on offer for a machine writer, and trap-with-report is the corpus's only carded post-violation observability story (C002-adjacent, round-2 mandated). Its burden-of-proof posture matches caveats.md's standing rule and N006's evidence that LLVM applies maximal ceremony to poison-generating relaxations. Its concessions were the most complete in the debate (overflow precedent cuts against it, elimination half-carded, relaxed trap ordering unspecified).
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The critic's attacks land. 'Forced by D1' overclaims: D1 forces check-OR-prove-OR-reject and explicitly delegates the escape-hatch question, so always-check is a policy choice whose D0 admissibility turns on the absent three-way benchmark — P1 concedes this while claiming 'forced'. The 'no third state' trichotomy is wrong for overflow: N002's unflagged wrap is a sound no-check-no-assume fallback the spine fully permits (the spine forbids assuming unstated facts, not declining to state them), and the only real-world precedent (Rust release-wrap) cuts against always-check there — though that precedent is itself uncarded and inadmissible as deployed. Its performance-recovery mechanism rides A005 read past its text: the card is unverified (absent from the 2026-07-02 verification set), says only that runtime checks 'influence' vectorization, was already discounted by round1-arrays-loops as vectorizer-internal speculation, and its loop-invariant pointer-range guard shape does not transfer to per-element bounds/overflow checks where P1's cost concentrates. 'Byte-identical semantics in all modes' is not yet a defined claim: it presupposes a sound relaxed-trap-ordering spec (absent; without it checked inner loops face a vectorization cliff under strict trap ordering) and concurrent-trap semantics (tasked to this debate, unanswered). Elimination rates are unmeasured and its fuel (range types) is an undecided, uncarded feature. It has no composition story for a sanctioned unsafe hatch if the safety-envelope debate lands one.

positionAssessments.position: P2 — Graduated T0-T3 (proven assumed everywhere; marked+proof-attempt-logged assume trap-free in release, debug-checked; unmarked unproven facts checked in release; conservative fallback at unverifiable boundaries)
positionAssessments.strengths: Only position that expresses per-fact-class decomposition, which the evidence says is mandatory (bounds, overflow, and alias disjointness have different checkability, fallbacks, and D1 criticality — the round-1 verdict's 'category error' point). Its T3 default coincides with the forced floor (unmarked unproven => checked in release), T1/T0 are shared and sound, and it degrades gracefully: if the escape-hatch ruling is zero-hatch, it collapses cleanly to proven-else-checked. The proof-attempt-and-log gate is the debate's one genuine design contribution to D1's delegated 'proof burden on AI writers' question — attempt-required/success-not-required is a coherent point between Rust's bare unsafe and SPARK's proof-or-reject, and D0 makes its ceremony admissible. Its concessions were candid (spine conflict, uncarded T2 for alias, attempt-gate unprecedented).
positionAssessments.weaknesses: T2 as written is illegal under decided law: the verification spine admits exactly two fact sources and says there is no third state; P2 concedes needing a formal amendment while its core argument asserts the log already IS lawful provenance — resolution by fiat, as the critic charged. It also pre-decides the escape-hatch question D1 delegated to the sibling safety-envelope debate. Its alias ladder exceeds the round-1 leading candidate it claims to instantiate: the amended candidate restricted the asserted tier to facts dynamically checkable in debug builds and explicitly held general scoped-noalias to proven-or-conservative; for that class T2 is logged-assume-with-no-check — a log audits, it does not test, so the 'ships contracts testing never executed' failure is relocated, not eliminated (no Miri/UBSan/TSan-class card exists for the debug leg). The overflow default binding is silently left open ('checked modes' parenthetical), so the policy is incomplete on one of the topic's three classes. Governance holes are real: adding an assume mark is not a contract edit, so contract-edit gating does not police the metastasis path, and prover-version churn makes the T2 residue unstable across toolchains. 'T1 sound without further rulings' is conditional on provisional C004, the unrun aliasing round 2, and the undesigned FFI wall.

positionAssessments.position: P3 — Prove-or-reject with explicit checked ops as the only opt-in (no compiler-inserted checks anywhere; residuals must be writer-spelled checked operations lowering to trap=abort)
positionAssessments.strengths: Maximizes the proven tier, the only region of the corpus with well-attested wins (F001, F006), and its truthful-lowering discipline discharges the F003/N002/N005 frontend obligations by construction rather than by promise — the cleanest satisfaction of the verification spine. It is correct that check visibility in the re-read artifact matters for a machine writer, correct that D0 genuinely reprices the historical SPARK usability objection, and honest that it hardens beyond the recorded round-1 lean. For overflow, round-2's one-mode-per-node law does most of its work: a checked node as the visible opt-in and proof-gated assume is nearly decided law already.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its central entailment argument is refuted by decided law itself: the round-2 MEMORY floor makes deallocation compiler-DERIVED but surfaced as explicit IR drop ops plus machine-readable reports — decided law's own template for compiler-inserted, artifact-honest operations. Canonical form therefore entails 'checks visible in the elaborated artifact' (which P1/P2 can satisfy), not 'no compiler-inserted check exists'. Combined with its own claim (1) that the accepted-program set equals the implicit rival's, the entire differentiator reduces to an EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED writer-behavior bet (restructure-toward-provable vs checked-op spam), with the workload census absent and discharge rates uncarded — under D0, where spelling is free, it is P1 with the elaborator's pen handed to the writer plus a psychology wager. Evidence hygiene is worse than acknowledged: N004 cited without its confirmed load/call-only scope limit, A003/A008 unverified, range types undecided. An SMT-grade prover in the trust path cuts against round-2's closed-decidable-checking posture, whole-program provability makes rejection nonmonotonic under distant edits (undesigned repair-loop divergence), and it is silent on both debts assigned to this debate (concurrent-trap semantics, FFI-boundary fact policy).

keyTradeoffs: Tested=shipped fidelity vs release performance: retaining every unproven check in release buys identical semantics, attributed aborts, and the only carded observability story, at a runtime cost that is unmeasured; any trap-free tier buys an unmeasured speedup at the cost of shipping IR contracts testing never executed. Both sides of the delta are unquantified — the three-way benchmark is the arbitrating artifact and does not exist.

Third-state legality is a normative prerequisite, not an evidence question: the verification spine ('no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed') reads as forbidding any trap-free unproven assume, while the same synthesis tasks this debate to 'design graduated obligations' and D1 delegates the escape hatch to the safety-envelope debate. Each position resolved this contradiction by fiat (P1 abolishes, P2 quarantines-by-assertion, P3 declares unnecessary); it must be ruled, not argued.

Fact-class heterogeneity forbids a uniform policy: bounds violations ARE memory unsafety with no sound fallback (check, prove, or reject — D1-forced); overflow has a sound spine-compliant no-check fallback (unflagged wrap, no nsw), making its check a correctness/performance policy, not a D1 obligation; alias disjointness is runtime-checkable only in A005's loop-bounded pointer-range form, so its general form admits only proven-or-conservative. One policy per class, not one policy for the topic.

Who spells the check: the MEMORY drop-op template makes compiler-derived-but-artifact-surfaced operations lawful, collapsing most of P1-vs-P3 into a near-free spelling choice under D0; the surviving difference is a writer-behavior experiment (rejection-pressure restructure vs checked-op metastasis vs silent insertion), not a semantics question.

Elimination-dependent performance vs predictability: every position's admissibility under D0 leans on check elision or proof discharge whose rates no card measures, and optimizer-dependent elision collides with the unresolved predictable-cost-model vs guaranteed-optimization-floor charter question — a reportable, guaranteed elision floor is the only route consistent with the dispatch verdict's hostility to silent optimizer-dependence.

Universal A005 dependence: all three positions reconcile checking with performance via one unverified provisional card whose text covers only loop-invariant pointer-range guards in vectorizer-internal speculation, already discounted by round1-arrays-loops; additionally, strict trap ordering under decided trap=abort makes vectorization of per-element checked loops semantically illegal absent a relaxed-ordering spec that exists nowhere — a cliff no position priced.
evidenceGaps: The three-way benchmark (UB-asserted vs runtime-checked+versioned vs proof-gated) on bounds/overflow/aliasing kernels — speed, code size, check-elision rates under closed-world + mono floors; named by round 1 as the single most decision-capable missing artifact and load-bearing for every position's D0 admissibility.

A005 verification and scope: the card carries no adversarial verification vote, its text says checks 'influence' vectorization (not that a passed guard legitimizes assume-grade facts), and whether guard-then-assume exists at all outside loop-invariant pointer-range checks is uncarded — prerequisite to every position's performance story.

Sound relaxed-trap-ordering specification against LLVM's speculation/side-effect model, and concurrent-trap semantics (abort ordering, report atomicity, FFI-introduced threads) — the latter explicitly tasked to this debate by round-2 and answered by no position.

Post-check fact recovery (whether llvm.assume or dominating checks recover nsw/inbounds-grade optimization; N004 confirmed inapplicable to arithmetic results) and the compiler-inference fourth channel — either could reprice the entire debate.

Language-precedent cards: Rust release bounds-check policy and get_unchecked density/incident history, Swift trap-always costs, Ada/GNAT overflow-check overhead, SPARK proof-discharge rates — currently deployed as load-bearing support by P1 and P2 despite being absent, which caveats.md renders inadmissible.

Debug-mode dynamic checker coverage (Miri/UBSan/TSan class) for noalias/inbounds/nsw assumes — decides whether any marked-assume tier has a debug-check leg for the high-value fact classes; without it T2 has no middle rung where it matters.

The bounds-subsumes-overflow / firewall-overflow-from-index-arithmetic composition analysis — demanded by round-1 numerics, listed as a gap by all three advocates, performed by no one across three debates; it sizes real check density and decides whether overflow can be treated as a non-D1-critical policy class.

verdicts.leadingCandidate: Proven-else-checked core with artifact-surfaced checks, per fact class — a synthesis, not any position as written. (a) Compiler/prover-proven facts are assumed in every build mode at zero cost (shared tier, F001/F006/C004). (b) For D1-critical checkable facts (array bounds; alias-disjointness guards where a check form exists), every unproven fact carries a runtime check in ALL build modes lowering to the decided trap-with-report=abort; elimination only by proof; this is forced by D1 + the verification spine + trap=abort, not chosen. (c) All checks — writer-spelled or compiler-derived — are surfaced as explicit ops in the canonical elaborated artifact plus machine-readable per-function check reports, on the round-2 MEMORY drop-op template; this satisfies P3's artifact-honesty requirement without source-level rejection, and check-density/elision reports become the lint-class containment channel. (d) Where no check form exists (general non-loop-bounded noalias) and at FFI/int-ptr boundaries: conservative no-emit fallback, per the round-1 candidate and threat model. (e) Integer overflow is a policy class, not a D1 class: named modes are already law, unproven nsw emission is forbidden by the spine, unflagged wrap is a legal spine-compliant fallback, and the bare-'+' default binding stays with the open numeric-semantics default question. (f) Any trap-free marked-assume tier (P2's T2) is REMANDED to the safety-envelope/escape-hatch debate: it may exist only if that debate lands a hatch, a formal verification-spine amendment defines logged-assume as a quarantined provenance class, mark-addition becomes a contract-edit-gated operation, and a carded debug-check mechanism exists for the fact class; absent all four, the policy is proven-else-checked with no third state.
verdicts.rationale: Decisive where law forces: D1 is non-debatable, and for bounds and alias-guard facts there is no sound fallback — an unproven fact must be checked, proven, or the program rejected; ambient assumption is excluded by the verification spine, and trap=abort fixes the failure semantics. That floor is benchmark-robust: even a catastrophic check-cost measurement cannot reopen ambient assume, only route pressure into proof/restructure or the delegated escape hatch. Within that floor, the critic's drop-op observation is dispositive against the debate's loudest disagreement: decided law already contains a template for compiler-derived operations surfaced explicitly in the artifact, so P3's 'no compiler-inserted check' does not follow from canonical form, and P1-vs-P3 collapses to a near-free spelling choice plus an untested writer-behavior bet — hence a synthesis candidate rather than a winner. Equally decisive: the topic bundles heterogeneous classes, and overflow is not D1-critical (wrap is a sound, spine-legal fallback), so always-check-everything and prove-everything are both category errors as uniform policies; the policy must be stated per class, which only P2's skeleton could express. Honest where evidence is thin: the residue that genuinely separates the positions — a trap-free marked-assume tier in release — is illegal under the spine as written, deliberately delegated by D1 to the safety-envelope debate, and unquantified in both cost and benefit (no benchmark, no elision rates, no post-check-recovery card, A005 unverified and shape-mismatched, all writer-behavior claims EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED); ruling on it here would be fiat, so it is remanded with explicit preconditions instead of decided. positionAssessments.position: Position 1 — Memory quarantine (copies + opaque handles; foreign arena; no LangHeap pointer crosses)
positionAssessments.strengths: Its core insight survives critique intact: because retention by foreign code is unverifiable (threat-model FFI row: verify 'no'), attenuation is monotone and permanent, so the only sound shapes are withdrawal or emptiness of the quantification domain. Its residency mechanism is the piece decided floors actually ratify — 'foreign-shared' as a writer-stated, allocation-site storage contract is one field on the existing round-2 memory floor, and it is exactly what P3's demotion becomes once repaired to respect the explicit-storage-contract and zero-cross-boundary-inference floors. It converts the boundary obligation into a syntactic check (no lang pointer in extern signatures) rather than a trusted semantic claim, which is the only shape compatible with the verification spine as written. Concessions were unusually honest (copy costs unquantified, ABI-axiom floor, erosion pressure, provenance coupling), and its framing of arena residency as 'relocating and pricing the tradeoff per-object by explicit writer choice' is the correct honest core.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The headline theorem — attenuation identically zero, domain empty — is falsified by the position's own machinery, as the critic showed: copy buffers must be arena-resident (an unstated, unpriced double-copy discipline) or LangHeap becomes retainable; exported callback env-structs live in LangHeap under the round-2 closures floor; the handle table is a shared mutable language-side root. The control channel is unclosed: a foreign thread executing a language callback touches LangHeap by construction, and 'C001/C004 quantify over language threads only' is design stipulation, not card content (verified — neither card scopes to language threads; C004's confirmed scope is safe-Rust discipline with incorrect impls UB). Citation defect confirmed: F1 in verified-findings.md has no negative-scope field. The sole answer to its own charter-collision risk (arena residency for hot interop data) rests entirely on an uncarded GPU/heterogeneous precedent and forfeits F001/F002/F003 payoffs on exactly the data D0 exists for. The arena is not safety-free even if fact-free: two language threads racing through accessors is a D1-banned race, so checked-access vocabulary returns. Finally, D2 (2026-07-02) amends the 'verbosity is free' premise: marshalling code is now paid from a finite program token budget, unpriced.

positionAssessments.position: Position 2 — Declared trust contracts (per-extern effect/alias/lifetime/thread frames; conservative undeclared default; provenance-tainted trusted class)
positionAssessments.strengths: Its declaration VOCABULARY is the durable contribution: the threat-model FFI row names 'boundary contracts / conservative fallback' as the remedy verbatim, round2-modules line 25 makes contract-bearing signatures non-optional, and even P3 concedes any future scoped-escape relaxation must route through marked-assertion machinery — so every position eventually needs P2's schema (reads/writes/escapes/threads/no-unwind/re-entry). It is right that an ambient ABI-trust floor exists under every rival and that the ownership floor forces stating the post-call borrow state of anything passed out. C004's 2026-07-02 confirmation strengthens its best precedent: the reference bar itself ships with declared-surface residual unsoundness of exactly this shape. It is the only position with any audit/attribution story, and the only one that names process/SFI isolation as an expressible bottom tier — the sole mechanism that would strengthen rather than restate the D1 envelope.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its legality claim fails as stated: 'exactly one sanctioned third class' is unsupported — the verification spine verbatim denies any third state, so the position needs a formal spine amendment it pretends already exists, and it repurposes round2-modules line 25 while deleting that line's content (re-verified against BODIES every build — the redundancy IS the point; no body exists at the wall). It fails the admission condition of the round-1 tier it cites as license: marked assertions must be 'dynamically checkable in debug builds', and its own evidence-gap list concedes whole-execution wall properties (escape, thread-publication) have no carded runtime check and no sanitizer cards exist. Its forcing argument ('pure conservatism is inexpressible under the ownership floor') is refuted by the uniform post-state rule — treat every crossing as escape to pre-declared foreign-shared storage, which P1 and P3 both instantiate. The computable-taint auditability claim has no carded mechanism through lowering (misleading-comparisons #5; uncarded Rust-noalias incident history), and N001 implies the taint frontier can be the entire optimization horizon. Worst structural fact: the wall is the one place with no body to re-check a declaration against, so round2-modules' coherent-hallucination failure mode has, by definition, no redundancy channel here.

positionAssessments.position: Position 3 — Runtime barrier (conservative invalidation at each crossing; permanent demotion of escaped memory; sanitizer dev builds)
positionAssessments.strengths: Closest to what decided law already forces as the DEFAULT: the conservative-fallback branch of the threat-model remedy, trilaterally converged in round 1; C003 cards the barrier discipline as precedented production-compiler machinery, not novel apparatus; the omission-is-sound structure (F003 contrapositive, N001) gives crossings the correct failure asymmetry — imprecision costs optimization, not soundness (with the provenance caveat below). Its partition insight is the real cash value of the modules cut: the closed world makes the invalidation set bounded rather than total, preserving F001/F002-class facts at full strength over the FFI-unreachable interior. It preserves zero-copy with the dossier's named targets, and it composes cleanly with the abort-at-boundary unwind rule into one coherent wall semantics.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: 'Withdrawal is the unique lawful attenuation' is the mirror-image of P2's overclaim: the spine is a synthesis sketch item coupled to research_needed ir-strategy, not an enumerated floor, and line 23 of the same document says 'declared fact-attenuation'; its own concessions then reintroduce marked-assertion relaxations, reducing the disagreement with P2 to defaults and gating. Permanent demotion as specified violates the floors it invokes — a compiler-inferred, call-site-triggered rewrite of a writer-stated storage contract collides with the explicit-storage-contract floor, zero-cross-boundary-inference, and contract-edit gating, and is ownership-incoherent for borrowed crossings; repaired, it converges to pre-declared foreign-shared allocations, i.e., P1's residency. Its soundness story silently depends on the int-ptr/provenance ban it never states: without it, reachable-set completeness fails and 'imprecision never costs soundness' is false (threat-model row 2: 'often no'). C005 is overread (undef uses can be UB downstream; D1 forces atomic-grade demoted access regardless, which the risks section admits and the core argument denies). The cost headline is contradicted by its own concession that true declared contracts soundly recover optimization on fine-grained hot-loop FFI, and its staging mitigation IS copying. Sanitizer channel entirely uncarded and structurally partial. D2 also prices its 'verbose mechanical rewrites for free' claim.

keyTradeoffs: Facts vs copies vs trust — the actual triangle: residency/quarantine preserves F001/F002/F003 non-interference facts across every extern call for all LangHeap but forfeits all interior facts on foreign-resident data and pays unquantified copies; barrier/demotion preserves zero-copy but forfeits facts on the escaped set and pins motion at crossings; declared relaxations recover both at the price of an unverifiable trust surface with N001-unbounded blast radius when wrong. Zero cost quantification exists anywhere in the corpus to price any corner of this triangle (caveats.md).

Failure asymmetry is the decisive soundness variable: partition/barrier/quarantine imprecision degrades optimization only IF the foreign-reachable set is a guaranteed over-approximation — which holds only under a LangHeap int-ptr laundering ban or provenance rule (threat-model row 2). Declared-contract imprecision is a silent, non-local miscompile. This asymmetry is what D1 forces the DEFAULT to be withdrawal-shaped; it does not by itself forbid a checkable marked-assertion tier later.

Auditability vs nothing-to-audit: withdrawal designs have no trusted artifacts but also no attribution channel when the ambient ABI axiom is violated (foreign scribble manifests as a mis-attributed trap in innocent code); declaration designs have an enumerable audit surface but no carded taint mechanism through the pass pipeline. Post-violation attribution at the wall is undesigned under every position.

The D1 envelope is identical across all three positions — 'impossible in accepted programs, conditional on ABI-well-behaved foreign code and the (undelimited) runtime TCB', Rust's shipped envelope — so advertised D1-composition or D1-by-construction differentiators are category errors (critic confirmed). Only hardware/OS isolation (process, MPK, CHERI) strengthens the claim itself, and it has zero cards; only P2 even names it as an expressible tier.

Token budgets (new, unpriced by all parties): D2 (2026-07-02) amends round-2's 'verbosity is free' — quarantine's marshalling/accessor code and the barrier's staging rewrites are paid from the finite PROGRAM budget, while P2's declaration vocabulary is paid from the SPEC budget. The wall design now has a teachability cost dimension no advocate priced.

Procedural deadlock: round2-modules line 24 requires the attenuation design BEFORE the closed-world commitment hardens, while caveats.md's standing rule plus ffi_abi_runtime (missing_primary_source, priority-high) forbid promoting any design now. The only lawful resolution is a provisional skeleton with pre-committed reopen triggers, not promotion — and the synthesis line-23 ('declared fact-attenuation') vs line-33 ('no third state') contradiction must be resolved by formal amendment, since every position was built by reading only one of the two lines.
evidenceGaps: ffi_abi_runtime (backlog line 6): missing_primary_source, priority-high — zero carded primary sources on ABI, unwinding boundaries, GC statepoints, pinning, or C/C++/Rust/Fortran/Python-extension interop; per decision-gates this alone blocks design_candidate status for ANY wall design.

Zero quantified copy/marshalling/barrier/demotion/contract-relaxation costs, and no workload census of large-buffer in-place vs thin-edge FFI in optimizer-first code — the single most decision-capable missing measurement (all three positions concede it).

No cards on the direct precedent family: JNI local/global refs and GetPrimitiveArrayCritical, .NET GCHandle pinning, Go cgo pointer rules, Erlang NIFs/ports, Wasm linear-memory sandboxing, CUDA/HIP device-handle interop — simultaneously quarantine's best precedent and its best counter-evidence (historical pinning-escape-hatch erosion).

No sanitizer/dynamic-checkability cards of any kind: whether TSan/ASan/Miri-class instrumentation can falsify a wrong reads/writes/escapes/threads wall declaration, at what cost — this single gap decides whether P2's trusted tier can EVER meet the round-1 marked-assertion admission condition ('dynamically checkable in debug builds').
Provenance/int-ptr rules unanswered (memory-object-model.md open questions): load-bearing for P1's syntactic check AND P3's reachable-set completeness; without a ruling, no wall design is sound.

Foreign threads executing language callbacks: no card defines what C004-class capability predicates mean for threads the runtime did not create, whether callback entry needs a thread-identity check, or the env-struct residency of exported callbacks under the closures floor — the closed-world reopen trigger arriving through the wall.

Inbound direction entirely undesigned: no threat-model row for foreign-supplied values (uninitialized buffers, invalid representations, aliasing-contradictory out-params); D1 bans uninitialized reads, and language-side marshalling from a never-written foreign buffer is exactly such a read.

Handle-table/accessor mechanics uncarded (generation-check costs, ABA), and quarantine's own infrastructure depends on the uncarded interior-mutability vs shared-reference-noalias tension backlog item.

Rust noalias enable/disable/miscompile incident history (named gap in round1-ir-strategy and round2-modules): the closest real-world evidence for the fact-falsification class at stake and for whether declaration-derived metadata stays attributable through lowering.
C002's UB-vs-bounded race semantics for foreign-shared/demoted memory remains open and couples to research_needed concurrency-model; the wall cannot fully close before it.
TCB never delimited: allocator, scheduler/runtime, and OS syscalls are foreign code beneath LangHeap that do not cross 'the wall'; the ambient ABI axiom is really an unwritten TCB declaration.

All AI-writer behavioral claims on every side (marshalling verbosity acceptable, declaration hallucination rates, batching/staging discipline, drift in boundary code) are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — zero AI-codegen evidence in the corpus — and D2's token-budget pricing of boundary verbosity is likewise unmeasured.
verdicts.leadingCandidate: Composite conservative-by-default declared wall (P3's barrier as repaired by critique + P1's residency + P2's vocabulary at its conservative bottom): every extern crossing carries a mandatory machine-readable declaration frame (reads/writes/escapes/threads/no-unwind/re-entry) — satisfying synthesis line 23 and round2-modules line 25 — but the only verifier-admissible frame is currently the maximally conservative one, preserving the line-33 no-third-state spine; C003-style barrier semantics at every crossing; any memory foreign code may retain must be PRE-DECLARED foreign-shared at its allocation site under the existing storage-contract floor (P1's arena residency and P3's demotion, repaired, are the same mechanism — compiler-inferred call-site demotion is rejected as a floor violation); mandatory LangHeap pointer-integer laundering ban / provenance rule as a forced companion decision; foreign unwinds abort at the boundary. Two knobs remain genuinely open and go to research, not debate: (a) whether non-foreign-shared LangHeap pointers may transiently cross under a no-retention obligation, and (b) whether per-function trusted relaxations are ever admitted — lawful only through the round-1 marked-assertion tier and only if sanitizer-class dynamic checkability is first carded. The honest envelope statement ships with it: D1 holds conditional on ABI-well-behaved foreign code and a delimited runtime TCB, identical across all designs.
verdicts.rationale: The debate's real finding, surfaced by the critic and confirmed against the texts, is that the three positions are one design with different defaults: once P3's permanent demotion is repaired to respect the explicit-storage-contract and zero-cross-boundary-inference floors it becomes pre-declared foreign-shared allocation, which IS P1's arena residency, which IS P2's escape dimension at its conservative bottom; and the direct answer to the debate question is the same under all three — the wall prevents falsification of internal proofs by (1) the closed world eliminating every non-call channel (D008/D004/D007), (2) never letting the optimizer consume a fact whose truth quantifies over unseen foreign behavior (conservative frame + omission-is-sound, F003/N001), and (3) keeping foreign-retainable memory outside the proved fact domain by allocation-site declaration — while NOT preventing an ABI-misbehaved foreigner from scribbling on the address space, a residual every position shares and only uncarded hardware/OS isolation removes. D1 plus the round-2 floors force this skeleton decisively: the conservative default is round-1 trilateral consensus named in the threat model's own remedy column; the int-ptr ban is forced because without it barrier/quarantine imprecision becomes a soundness hole rather than an optimization loss; and trusted relaxations are currently inadmissible because they fail the round-1 marked-assertion tier's dynamic-checkability condition by P2's own evidence-gap list. But the outcome must be research_needed, not design_candidate_later: ffi_abi_runtime is missing_primary_source/priority-high and per decision-gates blocks promotion; every cost that would arbitrate the surviving knobs (copies vs barriers vs relaxations) is unquantified; the inbound direction, foreign-thread callbacks, and TCB delimitation are undesigned by all parties; and the corpus's own decided texts contradict each other at the wall (synthesis line 23 vs 33), requiring a formal amendment no verdict owns. The composite skeleton should be recorded as the leading candidate with pre-committed reopen triggers, which also discharges round2-modules' design-before-hardening ordering without violating the caveats standing rule.

positionAssessments.position: P1 — Maximal proof burden: proof-carrying source (writer emits SPARK-grade checked annotations everywhere; compiler is checker-only, never infers)
positionAssessments.strengths: Its core insight survives the critique and refutes P2's dissolution move: for checker-underivable facts (loop invariants, value-dependent disjointness, trip counts, ranges — exactly A005's consumer list), writer statement is fact-CREATING, the only route past the decidability cap, because verification is cheaper than synthesis; the spine's stated-and-checked arm exists for precisely this. Its soundness is invariant to the unknown writer error-rate ε — false facts are compile errors, never miscompiles — so its failure currency (repair iterations) is recoverable where P2's (forfeited facts) is not. It moves nearly all fact mass into the one tier all round-1 positions agreed is assumable in every build mode. Concessions were the most honest in the debate (weak-annotation equilibrium, checker-TCB growth, one-way door).
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Four landed blows, two fatal to the position as written. (1) The 'compiler never infers optimizer-consumable facts' pillar violates decided law, not an amendable edge: round2-metaprogramming/synthesis mandates 'facts about constant data are compiler-derived by inspection, never writer-asserted' — the opposite polarity for an entire fact class. (2) The canonical-form conflict is structural and unrebutted: invariant equivalence is undecidable, so 'one spelling per semantic construct' cannot extend to a proof surface; equivalent-but-differently-spelled invariants are distinct canonical bytes, breaking drift-diffing and content-addressed caching under regeneration-heavy writers. (3) Its economic premise 'every fact stated costs only tokens, priced at zero' is now contrary to directive law: D2 (2026-07-02) amends 'verbosity is free' — proof-carrying source maximizes both SPEC budget (an invariant/VC vocabulary must be taught in-context to a model with zero training data) and PROGRAM budget; no party briefed this. (4) Proof-of-consistency is not proof-of-intent: a dischargeability-optimizing writer selects invariants provable from the same plan that produced the code, making weakening-laundering the plausible equilibrium, so the claimed fact-density gain is an unproven upper bound purchased with the largest one-way door. Mechanism family has zero corpus cards (no Dafny/Why3/F*/PCC), and deterministic resource-bounded discharge is undesigned. Survives only as the maximal setting of an optional checked channel, not as a mandate.

positionAssessments.position: P2 — Rust-division: writer states intent at boundaries (decided floors); compiler carries the entire derivation burden; writer-stated facts beyond the floor are optional checked redundancy, never trusted
positionAssessments.strengths: It is the fixed point of decided law: boundary-explicit intent, zero cross-boundary inference, deterministic ambiguity-rejecting interior synthesis, optional cross-checked redundancy with mismatch-as-hard-error, and contract-edit gating are all already round-2 floors — this position adds nothing and removes nothing. It is the only regime whose every load-bearing mechanism has adversarially-verified existence proofs at the D1 bar, and the 2026-07-02 verification file STRENGTHENS it beyond its own brief: C004 is now CONFIRMED_WITH_AMENDED_SOURCES and D001 CONFIRMED (the position understated its own evidence). Deterministic accept/reject preserves content-addressed memoization and the machine repair loop. Post-D2 it has a structural advantage no one argued: compiler-carried burden spends compute, not context budget. It is forward-compatible with every experimental outcome — if redundancy-independence mandates interior annotations they move from optional to mandatory channel without re-architecture; a solver pass can be added later as a fact-promotion stage without changing the writer contract.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its central dissolution move — 'writer proof burden can NEVER buy trust, only redundancy' — is refuted for checker-underivable facts, where a statement is the enabling input that converts an underivable fact into a checkable one; the position therefore caps the reachable fact set at deterministic-analysis decidability, a PERMANENT performance forfeiture under D0's extreme-performance mandate that it prices at zero (conceding only 'restructure loops'). Its laundering argument is a category error (interior invariant weakening is strength-erosion, a performance event, not the soundness-laundering gating exists to stop). Its determinism indictment attacks prove-or-reject (F006-style), not P3's actual prove-or-check-or-omit design. The boundary-independence claim doing the mandatory-vs-optional work (caller/callee generated in different contexts) is itself an exempted EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED writer-behavior claim. D009 — its silent-degradation exhibit — remains genuinely unverified, and the effect-row leg of its intent floor has zero exemplar cards (Cyclone/MLKit/Koka/DPJ, a named blocking obligation). Frontier size past borrow-checkability is unmeasured and is the decisive variable.

positionAssessments.position: P3 — Solver-assisted obligations: writer states only the floor facts plus optional checked hints; compiler generates residual obligations; a budgeted deterministic prover promotes what it discharges; residue falls to runtime-check or sound omission
positionAssessments.strengths: Correctly reframes burden as an elastic dial that is sound at every setting: budget exhaustion moves an obligation from proven to checked to omitted — always a performance event, never a soundness event — and every carded fact channel has a sound omission fallback (N002 unflagged, N004, N005 non-inbounds). Its tier-3 fallback matches round-1 violation-semantics' amended leading candidate (A005 runtime checks + versioning), and it is the only position with a post-violation observability story (C002 precedent under trap=abort). Routing burden to toolchain compute is the one cost channel that remains cleanly admissible even after D2 (CPU, not context tokens). Its hint channel generalizes the already-decided redundancy-as-hallucination-checksum machinery, and hints, being solver-checked, can enlarge but never poison the proven set.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Three design holes, one internal contradiction. (1) 'The solver never touches the D1 envelope' contradicts its own payoff claim: if solver-discharged facts ever elide safety-relevant runtime checks (the check-elision payoff it prices), a solver soundness bug is a D1-class breach — the architectural fence is asserted, undesigned, and incompatible with the payoff; either forfeit elision or admit the solver into the D1 trust path. (2) The undischarged-hint semantics create exactly the spine-forbidden third state or nondeterministic acceptance across solver versions — breaking canonical determinism and memoization — and the position never legislates which. (3) Its distinctive tier has zero corpus cards: F006 evidences prover-enforced RESTRICTION (decidability bought by bans), not budgeted SMT discharge of arithmetic/disjointness obligations, and the single number its coverage story depends on (SPARK discharge rates) is listed as missing/blocking. 'Prove-or-check-or-omit exhausts the sound design space' overclaims (the offline-profile channel is alive; A005 is itself static speculation-with-fallback). TCB enlargement runs against the metaprogramming verdict's confirmed restriction pattern, and performance nondeterminism collides with the unresolved predictable-cost-model charter question.

keyTradeoffs: Fact-creation vs error-surface at the high-value frontier (the P1/P2 head-on collision the decided law does not settle): writer statement is the only route past checker decidability to the top-payoff facts (dependence-freedom, disjointness, trip counts, purity), but statement quality under a correlated, dischargeability-optimizing writer is unproven — 'stated-and-checked' is simultaneously the channel P1 maximizes and P2 caps at boundaries, and the verification spine blesses both readings.

Failure-currency choice under unknown writer error-rate ε: all three regimes are D1-sound by construction, so proof burden is the dial that selects WHICH currency is paid — recoverable compile-loop explosion (P1), permanent conservative performance forfeiture (P2), or trusted-computing-base growth plus nondeterministic performance (P3) — and the corpus cannot rank these currencies today.

Deterministic acceptance vs discharge power: borrow-checking-class analysis gives stable accept/reject, machine-actionable blame, and memoizable checking; solver assistance widens fact coverage but must be fenced out of the acceptance decision (fact-promotion only) or budget nondeterminism breaks canonical determinism — the hint channel is where this fence currently fails.

Canonicalizability of the proof surface: invariants have unboundedly many logically-equivalent spellings and equivalence is undecidable, so any mandatory proof-carrying surface conflicts with one-spelling-per-construct, drift-diffing, and content-addressed caching; proof density beyond the floor must live outside the byte-canonical source or under new undesigned spelling rules.

Post-D2 budget repricing (unbriefed by all parties): verbosity is no longer free — writer-carried proof burden spends finite SPEC budget (teaching the annotation vocabulary in-context) and PROGRAM budget (emitting it per code unit), while compiler/solver-carried burden spends admissible compute; D2 structurally favors compiler-carried derivation and makes maximal mandatory annotation density a directive-law problem, not just an unmeasured cost.

Uniform regime vs per-fact-class decomposition: bounds/overflow (per-operation checkable), loop-bounded disjointness (runtime-checkable via A005 only), whole-execution noalias and purity/laws (static-only evidence, no dynamic check) differ in derivability, statability, and checkability — round-1 already warned one policy per contract class may be a category error, and a per-class burden table is the likely true shape of the answer.

Monotone coupling with the escape-hatch sibling: higher mandatory burden, higher conservative-rejection rates, or budget cliffs all raise pressure into the unsafe/FFI region; deciding proof burden and escape-hatch friction independently can produce the worst composite (maximal burden + cheap escape reverses the intended fact-density gain).
evidenceGaps: Zero AI-codegen evidence cards anywhere in the corpus — every writer-behavior claim on every side (invariant quality, correlated wrongness, weakening equilibrium, repair rates on VC vs type-error vs restructure diagnostics) is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; the AI VC-repair-loop experiment is absent from the round-2 experiment program entirely.

verdicts.leadingCandidate: Amended Rust-division (P2) as the mandatory floor, with an elastic checked channel above it. Mandatory writer burden = exactly the round-2 decided floors and nothing more: intent facts (ownership/aliasing capability, effect row, numeric mode, ABI-visible layout) at every signature/interface/instantiation, plus writer-stated storage/lifetime contracts at allocations; the compiler carries all derivation; nothing writer-stated is ever trusted unchecked. Three amendments forced by the critique: (1) P2's 'statement never creates facts' claim is struck — a stated-and-checked writer channel for checker-underivable facts (loop invariants, ranges, disjointness, law obligations) is retained as OPTIONAL, per-fact-class, pending the extended redundancy-independence and proof-ladder experiments; (2) a deterministically-budgeted solver pass is admissible only as a fact-PROMOTION stage that never gates program acceptance and never elides safety-relevant checks until a proof-checker TCB design exists (P3 absorbed with its third-state hint hole to be legislated); (3) proof density beyond the floor is a tunable coverage/performance parameter, not a trust decision — P1 survives only as the maximal setting of the optional channel and is ineligible as a mandate (metaprogramming-law violation as written, non-canonicalizable proof surface, and D2's amended verbosity budget).
verdicts.rationale: Decisive where law forces it: D1 plus the verification spine settle the trust question completely — no writer-stated fact is ever load-bearing unchecked, so proof burden is not a soundness dial in any position, and the mandatory writer surface today can only be the already-decided floors, because mandating more requires writer-behavior evidence the corpus states it has zero of. That skeleton is P2, the only regime whose every mechanism has adversarially-verified existence proofs (F001 3-0; C004 and D001 now CONFIRMED per the 2026-07-02 verification file, which I inspected — the newly-verified cards strengthen rather than reverse the leader, distinguishing this from round-1 violation-semantics' research_needed posture), and the only one forward-compatible with every experiment outcome. But P2 cannot stand unamended: the critic's sharpest point is verified against the spine's own text — 'stated-and-checked' is fact-creating for checker-underivable facts, so P2's decidability cap is a permanent D0 performance forfeiture it never prices, and the highest-value optimizer facts live exactly there. P1's density and P3's solver are therefore retained as the elastic, optional, always-checked layer above the floor — P1 is barred as a mandate on three independent grounds (it violates the decided compiler-derived-constant-facts law as literally stated; a proof surface is non-canonicalizable, colliding with one-spelling-per-construct and content-addressed caching; and D2, dated 2026-07-02 in user-directives.md, amends the 'verbosity is free' premise this debate was framed on, converting its token-cost-zero economics into a directive-law violation no party briefed). P3's lattice is architecturally attractive and D2-friendly (compute, not context) but design-incomplete at two soundness-adjacent points (the check-elision/TCB contradiction and the undischarged-hint third state), so it enters as a fenced promotion stage, not a regime. Honest where evidence is thin: whether the optional channel becomes mandatory per fact class, how much the solver actually discharges, and whether an AI writer can supply useful checked invariants at all are the deciding variables, and they have literally zero cards — hence design_candidate_later, not a decision, with the choice of failure currency under unknown writer error-rate (recoverable iterations vs permanent forfeiture vs TCB growth) explicitly deferred to the experiment program.
